notes: Docs-only sample content for Lane A planning/content/operator support.
Uses current shipped enum values only.
Intended for manual entry, QA/demo setup, or future import/reference work.
Category references below use categoryLabel for readability; adapt to actual IDs if an import path is later built.

campaign.title: Downtown & School Access — Safety Map Feedback — Spring 2026
campaign.summary: This campaign captures location-specific safety and access feedback related to downtown circulation and school access, including direct public comments, open-house notes, email feedback, and staff-entered observations. The purpose is to surface recurring problem locations, access barriers, and improvement ideas that should inform project review and downstream reporting.

publicFacingDraft.headline: Share what you see, use, and need in this area.
publicFacingDraft.subhead: Help the project team understand safety issues, access needs, and location-specific opportunities. Comments are reviewed before they are included in planning analysis or project materials.
publicFacingDraft.moderationDisclosure: Comments are reviewed for relevance, privacy, duplicate content, and safety before they are included in project materials.

categories.label: Crossing safety
categories.description: Crosswalk visibility, crossing distance, turning conflicts, school pickup conflicts, and near-miss locations.

categories.label: Sidewalks and accessibility
categories.description: Sidewalk gaps, narrow walkways, ADA barriers, surface conditions, and curb ramp issues.

categories.label: Bicycle comfort and network gaps
categories.description: Bicycle stress points, missing links, uncomfortable crossings, and route continuity concerns.

categories.label: Transit access and stops
categories.description: Stop placement, stop amenities, crossings to stops, schedule reliability comments, and first/last-mile access.

categories.label: Parking, loading, and curb use
categories.description: Short-term parking, loading activity, pickup/drop-off conflicts, curb turnover, and delivery access concerns.

categories.label: Maintenance, lighting, and visibility
categories.description: Vegetation, pavement, drainage, lighting, signage, striping, and sight-line maintenance issues.

categories.label: Support / positive signal
categories.description: Positive support, reinforcement of proposed changes, or appreciation without a separate primary issue.

categories.label: Other / needs follow-up
categories.description: Useful comments that are still too broad, mixed, or ambiguous for a tighter category assignment.

items.title: Fast turns during school pickup
items.body: Cars turning right toward the school cut the corner quickly and kids are already in the crosswalk. Crossing guards help, but the conflict still happens most afternoons.
items.moderationNotes: Approved as direct place-based safety comment. Location precise enough for mapping.

metadata.locationNote: Corner by elementary school pickup zone

items.title: Missing curb ramp connection
items.body: The sidewalk route from downtown toward the school has a curb ramp mismatch and broken pavement, which makes pushing a stroller or wheelchair difficult.
items.moderationNotes: Entered from open house board transcription and retained as a direct accessibility concern.

items.title: Bikeway disappears at bridge approach
items.body: Riding into downtown feels comfortable until the bridge approach, where the lane narrows and drivers pass too close.
items.moderationNotes: Approved from resident email; summary kept close to original wording and geolocated from location description.

items.title: Need better bus stop lighting
items.body: The stop near downtown is dark in winter evenings and students wait there after activities. Better lighting and clearer crossing markings would help.
items.moderationNotes: Pending because stop location should be confirmed before final approval.

items.title: Delivery trucks block visibility
items.body: Morning delivery trucks often occupy the curb near the coffee shop and make it hard to see people crossing from the corner.
items.moderationNotes: Approved as curb-management issue with crossing visibility implications.

items.title: Trees block stop sign
items.body: Overgrown branches make it hard to see the stop sign from one approach, especially if you are unfamiliar with the area.
items.moderationNotes: Internal site note retained because it documents an observable visibility condition relevant to the campaign.

items.title: Support for calmer school frontage
items.body: Anything that slows drivers and makes the school frontage feel calmer would be welcome. Families walk here every day and the current setup feels too rushed.
items.moderationNotes: Approved as positive support signal without exact map location.

items.title: Possible duplicate of pickup conflict
items.body: Another participant described dangerous turning movements during dismissal at the same school corner mentioned elsewhere in the campaign.
items.moderationNotes: Flagged for duplicate review against earlier school pickup conflict record; retain if additional details are materially different.

items.title: Off-topic complaint
items.body: This project should also lower utility rates and fix broadband service.
items.moderationNotes: Rejected as off-topic for the campaign scope.

items.title: Crossing near library feels too wide
items.body: The crossing near the library takes too long for seniors and children, and drivers do not always yield when someone starts late in the cycle.
items.moderationNotes: Approved as direct crossing geometry and yielding concern.

items.title: Need clearer category before handoff
items.body: Several comments mention that downtown feels stressful for walking after dark, but the issue mixes lighting, visibility, and personal security concerns.
items.moderationNotes: Pending until operator decides whether to split into lighting/visibility versus perceived security theme.
